title: Sam Lokanc
text:
About
I am a Data Scientist based in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. I have recently graduated from the Master of Data Science program at the University of British Columbia. Prior to this, I received a Bachelor of Health Sciences degree in Bioinformatics from the University of Calgary.
In the past I have conducted research investigating the intersection of cancer and the immune system utilizing data-driven approaches. These experiences supplied me with a skillset that is generalizable to a wide range of exciting problems, such as wildlife conservation and epidemiology.
Feel free to check out some of my projects (both professional and personal) as well as my resume on this website!

                Unsupervised Machine Learning
                
              
            

            
              
                Completed as a capstone project during the Masters of Data Science Program at UBC in partnership with the BC centre for disease control. An unsupervised machine learning pipeline to extract useful diagnostic criteria from clinical notes (unstructured text data).
              
            
          

          
            

            
          

        
      
    
      
        
        

          
            
              Bird Acoustic Monitoring Pipeline
            

            
              
                
                Bash
                
                High Performance Computing
                
              
            

            
              
                A data pipeline to process large volumes of acoustic recordings. Generates species level predictions to assist in the monitoring of Vancouver bird populations and support biodiversity.
              
            
          

          
            

            
          

        
      
    
      
        
        

          
            
              Bird Acoustic Monitoring Dashboard
            

            
              
                
                Bash
                
                High Performance Computing
                
              
            

            
              
                A dashboard to visualize the outcome of the Bird Acoustic Monitoring Pipeline project. Provides digestible visualizations of species diversity, detection volume, classification confidence, and more. Assists in making results accessible to researchers investigating biodiversity and ecosystem health.
